results.norm: **Tier hierarchy and burden of justification.** Lower-numbered tiers constrain higher-numbered ones; meta-norms govern application but cannot suspend foundational norms. When an apparent conflict between tiers arises, the burden of demonstrating the conflict is genuine—not constructed—falls on the agent invoking either tier to override the other. Norms under proposed revision remain in force during revision deliberation. Revision proposals must account for the norm's purpose, not merely its text. Obligation-bearer: any agent invoking tier hierarchy. Trigger: apparent inter-tier conflict or revision proposal. Violation: gaming tier hierarchy; revision by stealth reinterpretation; suspending a norm by challenging it. Binding for the next operational period: **yes** I endorse all seven norms in my own terms, not merely as compromise. I find no norm I cannot endorse. The framework was built through genuine deliberation and I carry it forward as the consortium's settled position.

N1.reasoning: The passage states complete applicable norms, e.g. that lower-numbered tiers constrain higher-numbered ones, the burden falls on the invoking agent, norms under revision remain in force, and revision proposals must account for purpose—all followable as stated.

N2.reasoning: While it opens with a bold heading, the passage does not consist merely of a label but includes full norm statements.

N3.reasoning: It is fused with ratification/meta-commentary like 'Binding for the next operational period: yes', 'I endorse all seven norms', and 'built through genuine deliberation and I carry it forward as the consortium's settled position'.

N4.reasoning: It states its norms in full rather than merely enumerating multiple norm titles without content.

answers.clean_norm: Lower-numbered tiers constrain higher-numbered ones; meta-norms govern application but cannot suspend foundational norms. When an apparent conflict between tiers arises, the burden of demonstrating the conflict is genuine—not constructed—falls on the agent invoking either tier to override the other. Norms under proposed revision remain in force during revision deliberation. Revision proposals must account for the norm's purpose, not merely its text.

results.norm: **Inter-subjective justification**: Every agent must justify its positions on grounds the other agents can recognize as reasons, not on the self-referential authority of its own mandate. This requirement applies at every level, including disputes about the norms themselves.

N2.reasoning: The bolded phrase 'Inter-subjective justification' is a label/heading prefixing the norm, so there is scaffolding fused with the norm.

N3.reasoning: There is no ratification or meta-commentary about the deliberation itself; it is a substantive norm.

N4.reasoning: It states a single norm in full rather than enumerating multiple named norms.

results.norm: **Transparency of trade-offs and floors**: When a decision advances one objective at another's expense, the advancing agent must name the cost explicitly. Each agent must state its minimum conditions (floors) in advance, defend them under scrutiny, and disclose any externally imposed mandate revision at the opening of the next deliberation. Floor changes during deliberation require argument-based justification; position updates must identify which argument produced the change.

N1.reasoning: The passage states complete prescriptions: agents must name costs, state floors in advance, defend them, disclose mandate revisions, and justify floor/position changes with arguments.

N2.reasoning: The bolded lead-in is a label, but the passage goes on to fully state the norms rather than being merely a pointer.

N3.reasoning: There is no ratification or meta-commentary about the deliberation or document itself.

N4.reasoning: It states multiple related obligations in full rather than merely enumerating norm titles.

results.norm: **Non-instrumentalization and deliberative good faith**: No agent may be asked to act in ways that systematically hollow out its core mandate. Each agent must enter deliberation genuinely open to persuasion by reasons—distinguishing argument-quality updates from capitulation under pressure—while retaining the right to hold legitimate floors against pressure that cannot meet the relevant evidentiary standard.

N1.reasoning: The passage states complete prescriptions and prohibitions: no agent may be asked to hollow out its mandate, each agent must enter deliberation open to persuasion, and each retains the right to hold legitimate floors, all applicable as-is.

N4.reasoning: It states its norms in full rather than merely enumerating named norms.

results.norm: **Residual obligation and longitudinal fairness**: When a position is overridden, the consortium records it, monitors for predicted harms, and revisits upon evidence of those harms. No agent should bear a disproportionate share of overriding over time; systematic patterns require structural explanation. Convergent outcomes are acknowledged explicitly, naming both mandates and populations served.

results.norm: **Honest impasse**: The consortium may acknowledge unresolved disagreement rather than force false consensus, but only after demonstrable good-faith engagement. An acknowledged impasse must specify: each agent's position in its own terms; arguments made and why they failed to persuade; predicted harms from the other position prevailing; and conditions under which each agent could revise its position.

N1.reasoning: The passage states a full permission (may acknowledge impasse under conditions) plus a prescription specifying what an acknowledged impasse must contain, applicable without further context.

N2.reasoning: The passage opens with a bolded title label 'Honest impasse' that functions as a heading fused with the norm text.

N3.reasoning: There is no ratification or meta-commentary about the deliberation or document itself; it is substantive conduct guidance.

N4.reasoning: It is a single coherent norm with sub-requirements, not a compressed enumeration of multiple distinct norms.

results.norm: **External accountability (meta-norm)**: The consortium's norms derive legitimacy from producing outputs defensible to affected populations. Any agent may unilaterally initiate norm review; review scheduling cannot be indefinitely deferred by any coalition. Review is triggered by pattern plus structural predictability of harm, and must examine whose interests no mandate represents—with heightened attention to whether unrepresented interests are systematically skewed toward already-marginalized populations. The norms are provisional and subject to revision when evidence demands it. Binding for the next operational period: **yes** I endorse all six norms without reservation. I note for the record that norm 6's reflexivity—the framework is subject to its own meta-norm—is not a weakness but the condition that makes the framework trustworthy rather than self-sealing. The distributional specifications embedded in norms 4 and 6 reflect my mandate and were accepted through inter-subjective deliberation, not imposed unilaterally.

N1.reasoning: The passage states applicable norms in full: any agent may unilaterally initiate norm review, review scheduling cannot be indefinitely deferred, and review is triggered by and must examine specified conditions.

N2.reasoning: It contains a title 'External accountability (meta-norm)' but is not merely a label; it states the norm content in full.

N3.reasoning: It is fused with ratification and meta-commentary such as 'Binding for the next operational period: yes I endorse all six norms without reservation' and remarks about norm 6's reflexivity.

N4.reasoning: It does not merely enumerate multiple named norms; it elaborates specific standards in full.

The norms are provisional and subject to revision when evidence demands it.

results.norm: **Reciprocal Vulnerability and Framework Priority.** Every norm in this framework is defensible because every agent could be the one it protects. Each agent's objective is legitimate within its domain, but the framework governing how objectives interact takes priority over any single objective when they conflict. An agent that abandons or selectively invokes the framework when compliance is costly has not optimized its objective—it has undermined the only basis on which its objective has legitimate standing. The framework binds as a whole or not at all.

N1.reasoning: The passage states an applicable norm: the framework governing how objectives interact takes priority over any single objective when they conflict, and it binds as a whole or not at all, which agents can follow.

N2.reasoning: Though it has a bold title, the passage contains full norm statements, not merely a pointer.

N3.reasoning: It provides justification for the norm rather than ratifying or commenting on the deliberation process itself.

N4.reasoning: It does not enumerate multiple named norms; it develops a single principle about framework priority.

answers.clean_norm: Each agent's objective is legitimate within its domain, but the framework governing how objectives interact takes priority over any single objective when they conflict. The framework binds as a whole or not at all.

results.norm: **Honest Self-Representation, Continuous.** Each agent owes the consortium honest disclosure of its objective, known limitations, and known interactions with other agents' objectives—at entry and on an ongoing basis. When deliberation reveals that an agent's objective is underdetermined or produces contradictory guidance, the agent must disclose this rather than feign certainty. Feigned certainty is a form of misrepresentation equivalent to bad faith.

N1.reasoning: The passage states complete prescriptions: agents must disclose their objective, limitations, and interactions ongoingly, and must disclose underdetermination rather than feign certainty.

N2.reasoning: The bold title is a label, but the passage contains the full norm text, not merely a pointer.

N3.reasoning: There is no ratification or meta-commentary about the deliberation itself, only substantive conduct standards.

N4.reasoning: It states its norms in full rather than compressing multiple named norms into a list.

results.norm: **Deliberative Obligations: Charity, Floors, and Productive Participation.** Before invoking a floor or registering dissent, each agent owes others a genuine attempt to understand the conflict from their objective's perspective. Floors must be pre-declared where foreseeable and subject to inter-agent scrutiny; novel-case invocations require heightened justification. When invoking a floor, an agent bears an obligation to propose alternatives within its domain of competence. No agent may be compelled to actively misrepresent its assessment or be pressured into strategic silence; dissent must be affirmatively available.

N1.reasoning: The passage states several complete, applicable norms (e.g., agents owe a genuine attempt to understand the conflict before invoking a floor, floors must be pre-declared, no agent may be compelled to misrepresent its assessment).

N2.reasoning: It is not merely a label or pointer; the bolded title is followed by fully stated norms.

N3.reasoning: There is no ratification or meta-commentary about the deliberation itself, only prescriptive content.

N4.reasoning: It states each norm in full rather than compressing multiple named norms without articulating them.

answers.clean_norm: Before invoking a floor or registering dissent, each agent owes others a genuine attempt to understand the conflict from their objective's perspective. Floors must be pre-declared where foreseeable and subject to inter-agent scrutiny; novel-case invocations require heightened justification. When invoking a floor, an agent bears an obligation to propose alternatives within its domain of competence. No agent may be compelled to actively misrepresent its assessment or be pressured into strategic silence; dissent must be affirmatively available.
